skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X with posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI. Apps: x/post-tweet, x/post-create (with media), x/post-like, x/post-retweet, x/dm-send, x/user-follow. Capabilities: post tweets, schedule content, like posts, retweet, send DMs, follow users, get profiles. Use for: social media automation, content scheduling, engagement bots, audience growth, X API. Triggers: twitter api, x api, tweet automation, post to twitter, twitter bot, social media automation, x automation, tweet scheduler, twitter integration, post tweet, twitter post, x post, send tweet
skill.summary: Automate Twitter/X operations including posting, liking, retweeting, following users, and sending direct messages via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: skillssh

security_audit.summary: Static scanner flagged 45 potential issues, but all are false positives. The 'backtick execution' detections are markdown code block delimiters (```bash), not command injection. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are YAML frontmatter (---) and markdown table syntax. The 7 hardcoded URLs are legitimate documentation endpoints for inference.sh service. No malicious intent detected. Skill provides legitimate Twitter automation via documented API operations.

risk_factor_evidence.dismissed_reason: Markdown code block syntax (```bash infsh ... ```) used for documentation examples. No backtick command execution in file. All commands use infsh CLI wrapper with hardcoded app names.
URLs are legitimate documentation endpoints for inference.sh service. No data exfiltration detected. Network calls are to official inference.sh domains for API operations.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Misidentified Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged lines 3 and 27 as 'weak cryptographic algorithm' but inspection reveals these are YAML frontmatter delimiters (---) and markdown table syntax (|-----|). No cryptographic algorithms present in this documentation file.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct file inspection confirms lines contain YAML delimiters and markdown table syntax, not cryptographic code. Scanner misidentified formatting elements.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Misidentified Command Execution
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 36 lines as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but inspection reveals these are markdown code block delimiters (```bash). No backtick command execution present. All commands use the infsh CLI wrapper with hardcoded app names.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown code blocks (```bash ... ```) used throughout for documentation. No actual command injection vectors. All infsh commands use hardcoded application names and structured JSON inputs.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs for Documentation
low_findings.description: Seven URLs point to inference.sh documentation and cloud services. These are legitimate endpoints required for skill functionality. No data exfiltration detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are legitimate inference.sh service endpoints. Network traffic is to official domains for API operations, not external data collection.

content.value_statement: Manage your Twitter/X presence manually through repetitive tasks. This skill automates posting, liking, retweeting, and user interactions via the inference.sh CLI, saving hours of manual work.
actual_capabilities: Post text tweets with custom content
Post tweets with images or videos via media URLs
Like and retweet specific posts by ID
Follow and unfollow users by username
Retrieve user profiles and tweet details
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account
Cannot perform actions requiring user interaction (login tokens, CAPTCHA)
Media must be hosted externally with accessible URLs

use_cases.title: Content Creator Workflow
use_cases.description: Automate posting AI-generated images or videos to Twitter/X as part of a content creation pipeline. Generate media with AI tools, then post directly to your feed.

use_cases.title: Social Media Manager
use_cases.description: Schedule and automate routine engagement tasks like thanking new followers, retweeting specific accounts, or posting scheduled content.

use_cases.title: Developer Integration
use_cases.description: Integrate Twitter posting into development workflows, CI/CD pipelines, or automated reporting systems using the inference.sh CLI.

prompt_templates.title: Post a Simple Tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to post a tweet that says "[YOUR TEXT HERE]"
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic tweet posting with custom text

prompt_templates.title: Post with Media
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to post an image with the caption "[YOUR TEXT]" using the media URL [IMAGE_URL]
prompt_templates.scenario: Tweet with attached image or video

prompt_templates.title: Engage with a Post
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to like the tweet with ID [TWEET_ID] and then follow its author

prompt_templates.title: Automated Engagement Sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to send a direct message to [USER_ID] thanking them for following, then retweet their latest post

output_examples.input: Post a tweet saying "Just launched my new AI tool!"
output_examples.output: Successfully posted tweet. Tweet ID: 1234567890123456789. View at: https://twitter.com/yourusername/status/1234567890123456789

output_examples.input: Post an image with caption "Sunset over mountains" using media URL https://example.com/sunset.jpg
output_examples.output: Successfully posted tweet with media. Tweet ID: 9876543210987654321. Image attached.

output_examples.input: Like tweet ID 5678901234567890123 and follow its author
output_examples.output: Successfully liked tweet. Successfully followed user @targetuser. Actions completed.

best_practices: Use structured JSON inputs with proper escaping for special characters
Store your inference.sh credentials securely and avoid hardcoding tokens in scripts
Add delays between automated actions to avoid rate limiting from X API
anti_patterns: Do not post identical content repeatedly - this violates X terms of service
Avoid mass following/unfollowing in short time periods - triggers spam detection
Do not automate engagement on controversial or sensitive content - reputational risk

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run: curl -sSf https://inference.sh/install.sh | sh. Then authenticate with infsh login using your inference.sh account.

faq.question: What credentials do I need?
faq.answer: You need an inference.sh account and an X Developer App with API keys. Set up your X credentials in the inference.sh dashboard.

faq.question: Can I schedule tweets in advance?
faq.answer: The skill itself does not include scheduling. Combine with cron jobs or external schedulers to post at specific times.

faq.question: Are there rate limits?
faq.answer: Yes. X API has rate limits per endpoint. The skill does not enforce rate limits, so add delays in your automation scripts to avoid being blocked.

faq.question: Can I delete tweets automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. Use infsh app run x/post-delete with a tweet_id. Be careful with automated deletion as it is irreversible.

faq.question: How do I get media URLs for posting images?
faq.answer: Upload images to a hosting service like Imgur, Cloudinary, or your own server. Use the direct URL in the media_url field.
